title: 2022 ESC Member Survey
excerpt: In advance of the Edmonton Squash Club 2022 Annual General Meeting that will occur on December 18 at 2 PM at the squash club, we would like to get feedback from our members about how they are using the club and what we should be working on next year. content:
In advance of the Edmonton Squash Club 2022 Annual General Meeting that will occur on December 18 at 2 PM at the squash club, we would like to get feedback from our members about how they are using the club and what we should be working on next year.

Thank you for taking the time to fill out the survey! If you have any questions, please contact Tyler Walsh at tyler@edmontonsquashclub.org.

2022 Member Survey

Survey for ESC Membership survey ahead of the 2022 AGM

Step 1 of 8

12%

1.1 Mission & Governance

ESC Mission Statement: “The Edmonton Squash Club is member-owned not-for-profit society that operates a sports facility where members can play squash. Our programs support both competitive and recreational play through leagues, tournaments, and other social activities. We promote the benefits of squash through community engagement

Strongly disagreeDisagreeAgreeStrongly agree
Strongly disagreeDisagreeAgreeStrongly agree
NB: Please contact the Club Manager (Tyler Walsh) or a board member if you are interested in actively participating as a volunteer on the ESC Board director, a working committee, or an event committee.

1.2 Club Facilities

The ESC facilities were built in 2015 supporting 4 singles courts, a doubles court, fitness room, exercise equipment, food and beverage, and locker room services. The management team is responsible for ensuring members have safe access to our facilities and that facilities are well maintained and support the needs our membership. Note that the facilities are currently managed with our limited financial resources.
